question: A and B start walking from P towards Q. Distance between P and Q is 120 km. Speed of A is 24 km/h less than that of B. After reaching Q, B returns immediately to P and meets A at a distance of 30 km from Q. What is the speed of A?
A while going to school reduces his speed by 3/11 of his actual speed and reaches 12 minutes late. Find A's actual time.
A while going to school increases his speed to 12/7 of his actual speed. He reaches his school 30 minutes early. Find A's actual speed if the distance between his house and school is 18 km.
A while going to school increased his speed to 9/5 of his initial speed and reaches in 7 hours 30 minutes. If A had walked at his initial speed, then how much more time would he have taken?

A bus runs 37.5% faster than a car. Both start at the same time from P and reach at Q at the same time because there was a halt of 36 minutes for bus. Find out the speed of car if the distance between P and Q is 55 km.
In covering a distance of 3600 km, A takes 5 hours more than B. If A increased his speed by 25%, then he would have taken 4 hours less than B. Find the speed of B (in km/hr).

A arrives at a place 24 minutes earlier than scheduled time if he rides his bike at 72 km/h. If he rides his bike at 54 km/h, he arrives 16 minutes late. What is the distance of the place from his starting point?

If A runs at 45 km/h, then he arrives at a certain place at 11:00 p.m. But if he increases his speed by 15 km/h, then he reaches there at 9:00 p.m. At what speed must he run to get there at 6:00 p.m.?

A bus travels from X to Y at a constant speed. If its speed were increased by 15 km/h, it would have taken 2 hours less to cover the distance. It would have taken 1 hour more if the speed were decreased by 5 km/h. What is the distance between X and Y?
